pi_items.source: Task description instructs saving the project to this path.
Task description instructs creating this text file.
Computed via independent Python/R execution on generated SleepWide.csv (comparing Drug2 - Drug1; comparing Drug1 - Drug2 yields -4.062, both verify the absolute magnitude)
Computed via independent Python/R execution on 10 pairs from generated SleepWide.csv
Computed via independent Python/R execution on generated SleepWide.csv
Computed absolute mean difference via independent Python/R execution
Computed absolute Cohen's d via independent Python/R execution
Computed Shapiro-Wilk test on paired differences via independent Python/R execution. The claim of 0.175 is contradicted by the actual data.

privileged_info_summary: The task asks the agent to perform a Paired Samples T-Test on the classic Sleep dataset. We independently computed the values on the provided dataset. The absolute T-statistic is 4.062 with 9 degrees of freedom, producing a p-value of 0.00283. The absolute mean difference is 1.58, and Cohen's d is 1.285. However, the Shapiro-Wilk test on the paired differences yields a p-value of 0.0333, contradicting the task's claim of 0.175. The agent should find 0.033 for the normality test and the rest of the expected values.
